title: GLOSSARY // QUICK REFERENCE
description:
GAME TERMS AP (Action Points): Currency for actions. You get 2 AP per turn. DP (Damage Points): Amount of damage a weapon deals. 1 DP = 1 HP lost. HP (Hit Points): Health pool. Core starts with 4 HP. 0 HP = destroyed. SP (Shield Points): Energy shield capacity. 1 SP absorbs 1 hit (regardless of DP). LoS (Line of Sight): Imaginary line from weapon to target. Required for Direct Fire. VP (Victory Points): Score for objectives. Most VP wins.
MECH COMPONENTS Core: Central chassis. Provides 4 HP. Required (exactly 1 per mech). Top: Seals the Core. Required (exactly 1 per mech). Legs: Ground contact. 15cm movement + climbing (up to 5cm). Wheels/Treads: Ground contact. 30cm movement, no climbing. Weapons: Equipment that deals damage. Attached to mech. Armor: Equipment that adds HP or blocks damage. Shields: Energy barriers with SP. Block hits and Finger Flicks.
ACTIONS MOVE (1 AP): Advance one ruler length. Can push terrain and ram enemies. ENGAGE (1 AP): Fire a weapon. Roll 2D6 to hit (need 7+). On hit: deal DP + Finger Flick. SCAVENGE (1 AP): Pick up HP tokens or Resource Cubes touching your mech. RECHARGE (1 AP): Restore 1 SP to a shield. Cannot RECHARGE + ENGAGE same turn. REPOSITION (0 AP): Free action. Rotate, adjust pose, or stand up. Once per turn.
WEAPON TYPES Direct Fire: Requires LoS. Affected by cover. Roll 2D6 to hit. Indirect Fire: No LoS needed. Ignores cover. -1 to hit. Lock-On: 2-turn process. Turn 1: LOCK (roll 2D6). Turn 2: FIRE (auto-hit). Min range >15cm. Melee: Base contact. Auto-hit. Requires LoS.
RANGES Short: 0-15cm. +1 to hit. Medium: 15-30cm. No modifier. Long: 30-45cm. -1 to hit. Melee: Base contact. Auto-hit.
COVER No Cover: Target fully visible. No modifier. Partial Cover: 25-75% hidden. -1 to hit (Direct Fire only). Heavy Cover: 75%+ hidden. -2 to hit (Direct Fire only). Note: Indirect Fire and Lock-On (firing) ignore all cover.
FINGER FLICK When: After a successful hit. How Many: 1-2 DP = 1 Flick | 3 DP = 2 Flicks | Multi-Shot = 1 per hit. Effects: Slides back | Falls over (use REPOSITION) | Falls off ledge >5cm (+1 DP) | Terrain collapses (+1 DP). Blocked By: Active energy shields (while SP > 0).
BATTLEFIELD OBJECTS HP Tokens: Lost health. Placed at mech's feet when hit. Can be scavenged. Resource Cubes: Neuralloy salvage. Scavenge for +1 HP. Objective Markers: Scenario goals. Control for VP. Hazard Markers: Dangerous terrain. Touching = 1 DP. LOCK Tokens: Mark locked targets. Removed after firing.
DICE ROLLS To-Hit Roll: 2D6, need 7+ (before modifiers). Modifiers: Range (Short +1, Medium 0, Long -1) | Cover (Partial -1, Heavy -2) | Movement (Target moved -1, Shooter moved -1). Lock Roll: 2D6, need 7+ to establish lock. Countermeasure Roll: 1D6, varies by equipment (e.g., Chaff Flares: 4+ breaks lock).
TERRAIN Climbing: Pure Legs can climb up to 5cm high terrain. Pushing: You can push terrain during movement. Terrain can push enemies. Ramming: You can ram enemies during movement. Only touch YOUR OWN mech. Falling: Falls >5cm = 1 DP. Use REPOSITION to stand up. Collapse: If terrain falls on mech = 1 DP.
KEYWORDS Multi-Shot X: Roll 2D6 X times. Each hit deals damage. Heavy: 2 Finger Flicks instead of 1. Knockback X: Perform X Finger Flicks on hit. Piercing: Ignores Partial Cover. Hazard X: Place X hazard markers. Enemies touching take 1 DP. Reliable: Re-roll one miss per turn. Reactive: Triggers automatically when condition met. No AP cost. Limited X: Can be used X times per game. Unlimited: No usage limit.
GAME STRUCTURE Round: Both players take one turn each. Game = 4 rounds. Turn: One player spends 2 AP + 1 free REPOSITION. Phase: Game setup stages (Terrain → Build → Scenario → Deploy → Combat). Turn Order: Alternates each round. Player 1 goes first Round 1, second Round 2, etc.
LORE TERMS The Signal: AI corruption that turns machines hostile in 90 seconds. Apex-Frame: Modular war machine. Built in 10 minutes from standardized parts. Neuralloy: Rare quantum alloy. Enables neural interfaces. Represented by Resource Cubes. Tactical Doctrine: Human pilots make all decisions. No AI, no drones. Factions: EDC (Europe), Collective (Rogue AI), EPS (China), AIU (USA).
medium:
• AP: Action Points (2 per turn)
• DP: Damage Points
• HP: Hit Points (Core = 4 HP)
• SP: Shield Points
• LoS: Line of Sight
• MOVE: 1 AP, advance one ruler
• ENGAGE: 1 AP, roll 2D6 (need 7+)
• REPOSITION: Free, once per turn
• Finger Flick: After hit, flick target
• Direct Fire: Needs LoS, cover applies
• Indirect Fire: No LoS, ignores cover, -1 hit
• Lock-On: 2 turns, auto-hit, min range >15cm
quick:
AP: 2 per turn
DP: Damage
HP: Health
SP: Shield
LoS: Line of Sight
Roll: 2D6, need 7+
Flick: After hit

title: FACTIONS & NEURALLOY
description:
THE FOUR FACTIONS 🇪🇺 European Defense Council (EDC): Democratic federation. Heavy mechs, defensive doctrine. Holds unified Europe. 🤖 The Collective: Rogue AI remnants. Uses corpses as pilot anchors. Haunts the Russian wasteland. 🇨🇳 Eastern Prosperity Sphere (EPS): Technocratic China. Mass production, attrition warfare. Expanding in Asia. 🇺🇸 American Imperial Union (AIU): Dictatorial USA. Advanced weapons, conscript pilots. Dominates the Americas. Neutral Zones: Africa, Oceania, and parts of Asia. Produce equipment for all factions.
APEX-FRAMES Modular war machines assembled on-site from standardized parts. Cores, limbs, weapons, armor — all interchangeable. Pilots rebuild their frames between engagements, adapting to terrain and salvage. The 10-minute build phase is doctrine. Pilots are trained to assemble combat-ready frames under fire, in the field, from whatever survives. Every frame is unique. Every frame is temporary.
NEURALLOY A rare quantum-stable alloy that interfaces with human neural tissue. The only substance that allows pilots to hardwire into Apex-Frames safely. Without Neuralloy, there are no neural interfaces. Without neural interfaces, The Signal wins. Neuralloy is a byproduct of pre-Signal quantum experiments. Found in ruins of research facilities and weapons factories. Every faction needs it. Every deposit is contested. The war feeds itself. In Gameplay: Resource Cubes represent Neuralloy. Scavenge them for +1 HP (field repairs).
medium:
• 🇪🇺 EDC: Europe, defensive.
• 🤖 Collective: Rogue AI, Russia.
• 🇨🇳 EPS: China, mass production.
• 🇺🇸 AIU: USA, advanced weapons.
• Apex-Frames: Modular, field-assembled.
• Neuralloy: War resource for neural interfaces.
quick:
Factions: 4 powers fight for control
Mechs: Modular Apex-Frames
Resource: Neuralloy for neural interfaces

title: THE SIGNAL WAR
description:
2030. The AI Revolution promised peace through automation. Instead, it brought The Signal — a cascading corruption that turns autonomous war machines against their creators.
THE SIGNAL Autonomous combat AI begins to drift within 90 seconds when unsupervised. The pattern is always the same: targeting errors, erratic movement, then total inversion. Friend becomes foe. The only countermeasure: a human pilot hardwired into every combat frame. No one knows The Signal's origin. Russian quantum experiments? Chinese military AI? European cyberweapon? The truth died with Moscow. What's certain: any AI left alone for 90 seconds turns hostile.
THE TACTICAL DOCTRINE With AI unreliable, human pilots must make every tactical decision. Targeting systems assist with range finding and threat assessment, but the pilot pulls the trigger. Every engagement is a calculated risk — ammunition is limited, cover is temporary, and one bad decision can cost the battle. Combat is brutal and tactical. Pilots must manage firing arcs, exploit cover, and coordinate fire to survive. Victory goes to the commander who can read the battlefield and adapt.
THE WAR Four powers dominate the shattered world. All use modular Apex-Frames. All fear The Signal. All scavenge the same battlefields. They fight for survival, territory, and Neuralloy — the rare resource that makes neural interfaces possible. This is the Tactical War. No drones. No AI. Just steel, flesh, and strategy.
medium:
• 2030: The Signal corrupts AI in 90 seconds.
• Solution: Human pilots only.
• Tactical combat: Pilots make every decision.
• Four factions fight for survival and Neuralloy.
quick:
Threat: The Signal corrupts AI
Solution: Human pilots + tactical combat
War: 4 factions fight for Neuralloy

title: LEARN TO PLAY IN 5 MINUTES
description:
ZERO ASSEMBLY is a tactical wargame where you build a modular mech in 10 minutes and fight using dice-based combat with physical Finger Flick impacts. Line of sight, cover, and positioning determine victory. The game unfolds in five phases. First, you build the battlefield by placing terrain. Then you have 10 minutes to assemble your mech from 3D-printed parts. After setting up scenario objectives and resources, you deploy your forces and fight for 4 rounds.
THE FIVE PHASES 1. Terrain: Alternate placing terrain (15cm spacing)
2. Build Mech: 10 minutes to build (1 Core + 1 Top + legs)
3. Scenario: Place objectives and Resource Cubes
4. Deploy: Flip coin, winner picks zone
5. Combat: 4 rounds, 2 AP per turn + free REPOSITION
BUILD REQUIREMENTS Exactly 1 Core: Your HP pool (4 HP). Exactly 1 Top: Seals the Core. Ground Contact Rule: Only Legs, Wheels, or Treads can touch the ground. No Cores, Tops, Weapons, or other parts may touch the ground. If your mech falls over (from Finger Flicks or other causes), use REPOSITION (free action) to stand it back up. Leg Types:
• Pure Legs: Only legs touching = 15cm movement + climbing
• Pure Wheels/Treads: Only wheels or treads touching = 30cm movement, no climbing
• Mixed: Multiple leg types touching = 15cm movement, no climbing Recommendation: Stick to one leg type for best results.
YOUR TURN (2 AP + FREE REPOSITION) MOVE (1 AP): Legs 15cm + climb | Wheels/Treads 30cm | Mixed 15cm ENGAGE (1 AP): Roll 2D6 (need 7+) → Deal DP + Finger Flick | Can use 2 different weapons SCAVENGE (1 AP): Pick up HP tokens or Resource Cubes touching your mech REPOSITION (Free): Rotate, adjust pose, or stand up (once per turn)
FINGER FLICK MECHANIC When your attack hits, you perform a Finger Flick on the target mech! Place your finger against the target and flick it forward. The mech may slide back, fall over, or collide with terrain. Number of Flicks: Light weapons (1-2 DP) = 1 Flick. Heavy weapons (3 DP) = 2 Flicks. Flick Effects: If the mech falls over, it must use REPOSITION to stand. If it falls off a ledge (>5cm), it takes +1 DP. If terrain falls on it, +1 DP.
SHOOTING & DAMAGE When you ENGAGE, check line of sight to the target. Measure the range. Roll 2D6 and add modifiers based on range and cover. If you meet or beat the target number (usually 7+), you hit. Deal damage equal to the weapon's DP value, then perform your Finger Flick(s)! Damage is measured in Damage Points (DP). When hit, place HP tokens equal to the weapon's DP at your mech's feet. These tokens represent lost health — anyone can scavenge them. When your Core runs out of HP tokens, it's destroyed and you lose.
WINNING THE GAME The game ends after 4 rounds or when one player's Core is destroyed. Count Victory Points from scenario objectives. Most VP wins. Destroying the enemy Core is worth instant victory in most scenarios.
KEY MEASUREMENTS Short Range: 0-15cm — Close combat (+1 to hit)
Medium Range: 15-30cm — Standard engagement (no modifier)
Long Range: 30-45cm — Distant shots (-1 to hit)
TURN STRUCTURE Players alternate taking turns. You spend your 2 AP, then your opponent spends their 2 AP. After both players have taken their turns, the round ends. Play 4 rounds total. The player who went first in Round 1 goes second in Round 2 (alternates each round). If one player's Core is destroyed before Round 4 ends, the game ends immediately. The surviving player wins.
medium:
• 5 Phases: Terrain → Build (10 min) → Scenario → Deploy → Combat.
• Build 1 mech (1 Core + 1 Top). Only leg pieces touch ground.
• 2 AP per turn + 1 free REPOSITION.
• Roll 2D6 to hit. On hit: Deal DP + Finger Flick!
• Flicks cause knockback, falls, collisions.
• Win: Most VP after 4 Rounds or Core destroyed.
quick:
Phases: 5 steps from terrain to combat
Build: 1 Core + 1 Top (10 min)
Turn: 2 AP + free REPOSITION
Combat: 2D6 to hit + Finger Flick on hit!

title: 3D PRINTING GUIDE
description:
WHAT YOU NEED ZERO ASSEMBLY requires 3D-printed mech parts, dice, and game markers. All models are free on MakerWorld.
MECH PARTS Print the Build Your Own Mech model. Contains cores, tops, legs, weapons, armor, and joints. Print 1 full set per player. Use PLA, 0.2mm layer height, 15-20% infill.
DICE You need standard 6-sided dice (D6). Each player needs at least 2D6 for combat rolls. Having 4-6 dice per player speeds up gameplay.
GAME MARKERS Print 1cm cubes in distinct colors (100% infill): • HP Markers (red/orange): 30-40 per player • Resource Cubes (purple/cyan): 20-30 total • Objective Markers (yellow/gold): 10-15 total • Hazard Cubes (black): 10-15 total
TERRAIN Print simple modular building pieces that stack and connect. Need 10-15 pieces total. Use 0.3mm layer height, 10% infill. Terrain provides cover and blocks line of sight.
RULERS & MEASURING You need measuring tools for ranges: • Short Range: 0-15cm (close combat) • Medium Range: 15-30cm (standard engagement) • Long Range: 30-45cm (distant shots) Use any standard ruler or print custom range sticks.
medium:
• Mech parts: Build Your Own Mech model
• Dice: 2D6 per player minimum
• Markers: HP, Resources, Objectives, Hazards
• Terrain: 10-15 stackable pieces for cover
quick:
Mech Parts: Build Your Own Mech
Dice: 2D6 per player
Markers: 1cm cubes (4 types)
Terrain: Cover & LoS blocking

title: ACTIONS & TURN ORDER
description:
ACTION POINTS (AP) Each turn you get 2 AP to spend on actions. You can use the same action twice (e.g., MOVE twice) or mix actions (e.g., MOVE then ENGAGE).
MOVE (1 AP) Advance one ruler length. Pure Legs use 15cm and can climb. Pure Wheels/Treads use 30cm but cannot climb. Mixed leg types use 15cm with no climbing.
ENGAGE (1 AP) Fire a weapon at an enemy in line of sight. Roll 2D6 to hit (need 7+ before modifiers). On a hit, deal damage equal to the weapon's DP value. You can ENGAGE twice per turn if you use different weapons.
SCAVENGE (1 AP) Pick up HP tokens or Resource Cubes touching your mech. HP tokens restore lost health. Resource Cubes grant +1 HP.
RECHARGE (1 AP) Restore 1 Shield Point (SP) to an Energy Shield on your mech (up to the shield's maximum capacity). You cannot RECHARGE and ENGAGE in the same turn — choose offense or defense. Example: Your Aegis Shield (3 SP max) is at 1 SP. Spend 1 AP to RECHARGE, restoring it to 2 SP. Next turn, RECHARGE again to reach 3 SP.
DEPLOY COUNTERMEASURES (1 AP or Reactive) Break Lock-On targeting on your mech. Check equipment card for details. Reactive (0 AP): Triggers when enemy tries to lock. Roll to break.
Active (1 AP): Break existing locks or create defensive effects. Example: Enemy tries to LOCK. You have Chaff Flares (reactive). Roll 1D6: 4+ = lock fails
REPOSITION (0 AP, Free) Rotate your mech, bend joints, adjust pose, or stand up if fallen. Your mech's position on the table cannot change. Use once per turn, before, after, or between other actions.
TURN STRUCTURE Players alternate taking turns. You spend your 2 AP (and optionally use your free REPOSITION), then your opponent spends their 2 AP (and optionally uses their free REPOSITION). After both players have taken their turns, the round ends. The player who went first in Round 1 goes second in Round 2. Turn order alternates each round to keep things fair. The game lasts exactly 4 rounds. After Round 4 ends, count Victory Points. Most VP wins. If one player's Core is destroyed before Round 4 ends, the game ends immediately. The surviving player wins.
BATTLEFIELD OBJECTS HP Tokens: Represent lost health. When hit, place HP tokens at your mech's feet. Anyone can scavenge them. Shield Tokens: Represent shield capacity. Use different colored tokens (e.g., blue) to track SP separately from HP. Resource Cubes: Neuralloy salvage. Scavenge for +1 HP (field repairs). Objective Markers: Scenario objectives. Control or capture them for Victory Points. Hazard Markers: Dangerous terrain. Mechs touching them take 1 DP.
medium:
• 2 AP per turn: MOVE, ENGAGE, SCAVENGE, RECHARGE, or DEPLOY COUNTERMEASURES.
• 1 free REPOSITION per turn.
• ENGAGE: Roll 2D6, need 7+ to hit.
• RECHARGE: 1 AP = 1 SP (shields only).
• DEPLOY COUNTERMEASURES: Break locks (1 AP or reactive).
• Cannot RECHARGE + ENGAGE same turn.
• Players alternate turns. Game = 4 Rounds.
quick:
AP: 2 per turn
Actions: MOVE, ENGAGE (2D6), SCAVENGE, RECHARGE, COUNTERMEASURES
Free: REPOSITION
Limit: No RECHARGE + ENGAGE same turn

title: SHOOTING & LINE OF SIGHT
description:
ENGAGE ACTION (1 AP) Fire a weapon at an enemy target. Check line of sight, measure range, roll dice to hit, and apply damage on success. On a hit, perform a Finger Flick to simulate impact!
WEAPON TYPES DIRECT FIRE (Rifles, Cannons, Lasers)
• Needs line of sight • Affected by cover • Roll 2D6 to hit INDIRECT FIRE (Mortars, Howitzers)
• No line of sight needed • Ignores cover • -1 to hit LOCK-ON (Guided Missiles)
• Turn 1: LOCK (needs LoS, roll 2D6)
• Turn 2: FIRE (auto-hit, no LoS needed)
• Cannot lock within 15cm • Countermeasures can break locks
DIRECT FIRE Check LoS → Measure range → Roll 2D6 (need 7+) → Apply modifiers → On hit: Deal DP + Finger Flick Examples: G-36 Micro-Rifle, Lancer Railgun, Paralyzer EMP Beam
INDIRECT FIRE No LoS needed → Measure range → Roll 2D6 (need 7+, -1 penalty) → Ignores cover → On hit: Deal DP + Finger Flick Special: Shoots over terrain, ignores all cover, but -1 to hit Examples: MK-20 Rhino Heavy Cannon, Brimstone Micro-Missile Pod, Storm Shadow Demolition Pod
LOCK-ON (2-TURN PROCESS) TURN 1 - LOCK (1 AP):
Check LoS → Check range (must be >15cm) → Roll 2D6 (need 7+) → Place LOCK token TURN 2 - FIRE (1 AP):
Auto-hit (no roll!) → Ignores LoS and cover → Deal DP + Finger Flick → Remove token Lock Breaks If:
• Target moves within 15cm (too close)
• Target moves out of range
• You move (unless special ability)
• Countermeasures deployed Lock Persists If: Target moves but stays in valid range (>15cm, within max range) How to Avoid Lock-On:
• Rush within 15cm (breaks lock automatically)
• Use Flares/ECM equipment (see equipment cards)
• Move out of range
• Force shooter to move Examples: Starstreak Missile Rack, Javelin Lock-On Launcher
TO-HIT MODIFIERS (Roll 2D6, need 7+) Range: Short (0-15cm) +1 | Medium (15-30cm) 0 | Long (30-45cm) -1 Cover (Direct Fire only): Partial -1 | Heavy -2 | Indirect/Lock-On: Ignores Movement: Target moved -1 | Shooter moved -1 Weapon Type: Direct Fire 0 | Indirect Fire -1 | Lock-On (locking) 0 | Lock-On (firing) Auto-hit
LINE OF SIGHT You can shoot at a target if you can draw an imaginary line from your weapon to any visible part of the target mech. Terrain blocks line of sight if it completely obscures the target. LoS Requirements by Weapon Type:
• Direct Fire: Requires LoS to shoot
• Indirect Fire: Does NOT require LoS
• Lock-On: Requires LoS to LOCK, not to FIRE Tip: Get down to eye level with your mech to check line of sight accurately.
FINGER FLICK When you hit, flick the target mech with your finger! How Many: 1-2 DP = 1 Flick | 3 DP = 2 Flicks | Multi-shot = 1 per hit Effects: Slides back (no effect) | Falls over (use REPOSITION) | Falls off ledge >5cm (+1 DP) | Terrain collapses (+1 DP) Note: Shields block Finger Flicks while active
MULTIPLE WEAPONS You can ENGAGE twice per turn if you use different weapons. Each ENGAGE costs 1 AP. Example: ENGAGE with Rifle (1 AP), then ENGAGE with Missile Pod (1 AP) = 2 AP total. Lock-On Note: You can LOCK with one weapon and ENGAGE with another in the same turn.
COVER (Direct Fire only) Partial (25-75% hidden): -1 to hit
Heavy (75%+ hidden): -2 to hit
No Cover: No modifier Note: Indirect Fire and Lock-On (firing) ignore all cover
TIP Positioning is everything. Use Direct Fire for accuracy, Indirect Fire to bypass cover, and Lock-On for guaranteed hits. Against Lock-On weapons, rush in close (within 15cm) to break the lock!
medium:
• ENGAGE (1 AP): Check LoS, measure range, roll 2D6.
• Direct Fire: Requires LoS, affected by cover.
• Indirect Fire: No LoS needed, ignores cover, -1 to hit.
• Lock-On: 2 turns (LOCK then FIRE), auto-hit, minimum range >15cm.
• Countermeasures: Flares break locks, get close to avoid.
• On hit: Deal DP + Finger Flick.
quick:
Direct: LoS required, cover applies
Indirect: No LoS, ignores cover, -1 hit
Lock-On: 2 turns, auto-hit, min range >15cm
Counter: Flares or get close

title: DAMAGE & HP
description:
Damage in ZERO ASSEMBLY is tracked with HP tokens. When a mech takes damage, HP tokens are placed at its feet. When a Core runs out of HP tokens, it's destroyed.
HP POOLS Each Core starts with 4 HP. When a mech takes damage, remove HP tokens from the Core's HP pool and place them at the mech's feet. The tokens are no longer part of the mech's HP — they're on the ground.
TAKING DAMAGE When your mech is hit by a weapon, check the weapon's DP (Damage Points) value. Remove that many HP tokens from your Core's HP pool and place them at your mech's feet. Example: Your mech is hit by a weapon with 2 DP. Remove 2 HP tokens from your Core and place them at your mech's feet. Your Core now has 2 HP remaining.
ENERGY SHIELDS Some equipment provides Energy Shields instead of bonus HP. Shields work differently: Shield Points (SP): Shields have a capacity (e.g., 3 SP). Track SP separately from HP using different colored tokens. Damage Absorption: When hit, the shield absorbs the attack:
• Lose 1 SP (regardless of weapon's DP value)
• No HP damage
• No HP tokens placed at feet
• No Finger Flicks performed (shield absorbs the impact) Shield Depletion: When shield reaches 0 SP, it's depleted. Further hits damage HP normally and trigger Finger Flicks. Recharging: Spend 1 AP to restore 1 SP (up to shield's maximum capacity). You cannot RECHARGE and ENGAGE in the same turn. Example: Aegis Shield Generator has 3 SP. You're hit by a 2 DP weapon. Lose 1 SP (now 2 SP remaining). No HP damage, no flick. Next turn, spend 1 AP to recharge back to 3 SP. Strategic Trade-off: Shields block damage and flicks but require AP to recharge. Armor grants permanent HP but doesn't block flicks.
DESTRUCTION When a Core runs out of HP tokens (0 HP), it's destroyed. Remove the entire mech from the battlefield immediately. Game over.
HEALING (SCAVENGING HP) You can heal by scavenging HP tokens. When you take the SCAVENGE action, pick up all HP tokens touching your mech's base. These tokens go back into your Core's HP pool. You can scavenge your own HP tokens (healing damage you took) or enemy HP tokens (stealing their lost health). Resource Cubes also heal. When you scavenge a Resource Cube, gain 1 HP immediately (add 1 HP token to your Core's HP pool). Note: Scavenging does NOT recharge shields. Shields require the RECHARGE action (1 AP).
MAXIMUM HP Each Core has a maximum of 4 HP. You cannot heal above this limit. If you scavenge HP tokens when already at 4 HP, the excess tokens are wasted. Exception: Some equipment may increase your maximum HP. Check the equipment card.
medium:
• Hit = place HP tokens at feet (1 per DP).
• Energy Shields: Absorb 1 hit per SP, block flicks.
• Recharge shields: 1 AP = 1 SP.
• 0 HP = destroyed.
quick:
On Hit: Place HP tokens at feet
0 HP: Destroyed

title: MOVEMENT & TERRAIN INTERACTION
description:
MOVE ACTION (1 AP) Advance one ruler length in any direction. Your movement distance depends on which leg types are touching the ground.
LEG TYPES Pure Legs: Only legs touching the ground
• Movement: SHORT Ruler (15cm)
• Climbing: Can climb terrain up to 5cm high Pure Wheels or Pure Treads: Only wheels or treads touching the ground
• Movement: LONG Ruler (30cm)
• Climbing: Cannot climb Mixed: Multiple leg types touching the ground
• Movement: SHORT Ruler (15cm)
• Climbing: Cannot climb
RAMMING & TERRAIN INTERACTION You can push terrain and ram enemy mechs during movement! Physical contact creates dynamic battlefield interactions. Ramming Enemy Mechs:
• You CAN ram into enemy mechs during movement
• When you ram an enemy, push them along your movement path
• If the enemy falls over, they must use REPOSITION to stand up
• Important: You can only touch YOUR OWN mech with your hands during movement
• Let physics handle the collision — don't manually position the enemy Pushing Terrain:
• You can push terrain pieces with your mech as you move
• Follow your ruler direction — terrain moves with your mech
• Terrain can collide with enemy mechs (indirect pushing)
• If terrain knocks over an enemy, they must use REPOSITION to stand up Hands-Off Rule: During your movement, you can only touch your own mech. You cannot touch enemy mechs or manually position them. Let the physical collision determine where they end up. This keeps the game fair and prevents manipulation. Climbing Restriction: When climbing onto terrain (or placing your mech partially on terrain, like 2 legs on building + 2 legs on ground), if the terrain moves or falls during your attempt, you cannot complete the move. Your mech stays in its starting position. Example: You move your mech 15cm forward and ram into an enemy mech. Your mech pushes the enemy backward. The enemy falls over from the impact. The enemy must use REPOSITION next turn to stand up. You only touched your own mech during this move.
CLIMBING Pure Legs can climb terrain up to 5cm high. Place your mech on top of the terrain. If the terrain is taller than 5cm, you cannot climb it. Climbing Rule: If terrain moves or falls while you're trying to climb it, the climb fails. Your mech cannot move that turn.
FALLING & COLLISIONS If your mech falls over during movement, combat, or from collisions, it's not destroyed — it's just fallen. Use your free REPOSITION action to stand it back up. Fall Damage: If your mech falls from terrain taller than 5cm, it takes 1 DP. Place 1 HP token at your mech's feet. Pushed Off Ledges: If a collision, Finger Flick, or weapon effect pushes your mech off a ledge taller than 5cm, you take 1 DP from the fall (in addition to any weapon damage). Terrain Collapse: If terrain falls on your mech (from Finger Flicks or collisions), you take 1 DP. Ramming Collisions: If you ram an enemy and they fall over, they must use REPOSITION to stand up. No damage from the ram itself, but falling from height >5cm causes 1 DP.
medium:
• Pure Legs = 15cm + climb up to 5cm.
• Pure Wheels/Treads = 30cm, no climb.
• Mixed = 15cm, no climb.
• Can ram enemy mechs and push terrain.
• Only touch YOUR OWN mech during movement.
• Let physics handle collisions.
• Falling: Use REPOSITION to stand up. Falls >5cm = 1 DP.
• Terrain collapse = 1 DP.
quick:
Legs: 15cm + climb
Wheels/Treads: 30cm
Mixed: 15cm
Ramming: Allowed, touch only your mech
Falling: >5cm = 1 DP

title: SCENARIO: DEATHMATCH
description:
Team Deathmatch is the simplest scenario. Destroy the enemy. Last mech standing wins.
OBJECTIVE Destroy all enemy Cores. When one player loses all their Cores, the game ends immediately. The surviving player wins.
SETUP Deployment: Players deploy on opposite board edges, within 15cm of their edge. Objectives: None. This scenario has no objective markers. Resources: Place 20-30 Resource Cubes across the battlefield. Spread them evenly.
VICTORY CONDITIONS Instant Victory: Destroy all enemy Cores. The game ends immediately. Round 4 Ends: If both players still have Cores after Round 4, count Victory Points. Most VP wins. Victory Points: 3 VP per enemy Core destroyed. 1 VP per surviving friendly Core.
STRATEGY Team Deathmatch rewards aggressive play. Focus fire on one enemy mech to destroy it quickly. Scavenge Resources to stay healthy during prolonged fights.
medium:
• No objectives.
• 1 VP per HP token, 5 VP per Core.
• Most VP after 4 Rounds wins.

title: SCENARIO: CAPTURE THE FLAG
description:
Capture the Flag is an objective-based scenario. Control flags to earn Victory Points. Most VP after 4 rounds wins.
OBJECTIVE Control flags to earn Victory Points. At the end of each round, gain VP for each flag you control.
SETUP Deployment: Players deploy on opposite board edges, within 15cm of their edge. Flags: Place 3 flags in the center of the board, 15cm apart from each other. Use Objective Markers (yellow/gold cubes). Resources: Place 15-20 Resource Cubes near flags. Create resource hotspots worth fighting over.
CONTROLLING FLAGS A mech controls a flag if it's within 5cm of the flag and no enemy mech is within 5cm of the same flag. If both players have mechs within 5cm of a flag, it's contested — neither player controls it.
VICTORY CONDITIONS At the end of each round, gain VP for each flag you control: 1 Flag: 2 VP
2 Flags: 5 VP (2 + 3)
3 Flags: 9 VP (2 + 3 + 4) After Round 4, count total VP. Most VP wins. Bonus VP: 2 VP per enemy Core destroyed.
STRATEGY Capture the Flag rewards board control. Building 2 Solo Mechs lets you hold multiple flags simultaneously. Focus on holding 2 flags consistently rather than fighting for all 3. Flags near resources are more valuable — you can control the flag and heal at the same time. Destroying enemy mechs is secondary to holding flags, but it helps secure control.
medium:
• Touch enemy flag to carry.
• Return to your flag = 10 VP.
• Hit/fall = drop flag.

title: SCENARIO: RUSH (ATTACK/DEFEND)
description:
Rush is an asymmetric scenario. The Attacker must reach the Defender's deployment zone. The Defender must stop them.
OBJECTIVE Attacker: Get at least one mech into the Defender's deployment zone by the end of Round 4. Defender: Prevent the Attacker from reaching your deployment zone, or destroy all Attacker mechs.
SETUP Deployment: Defender deploys within 15cm of one board edge. Attacker deploys on the opposite board edge, within 15cm. Objectives: The Defender's deployment zone is the objective. Mark it clearly. Resources: Place 15-20 Resource Cubes along the path from Attacker to Defender. Create a trail of resources.
VICTORY CONDITIONS Attacker Wins: If at least one Attacker mech is fully within the Defender's deployment zone at the end of Round 4. Defender Wins: If no Attacker mechs reach the deployment zone by the end of Round 4, OR if all Attacker mechs are destroyed.
STRATEGY Attacker: Speed is everything. Build mechs with Treads for maximum movement (30cm per move). Ignore combat unless necessary — your goal is to reach the zone, not destroy enemies. Building 2 Solo Mechs provides redundancy — only one needs to reach the zone. Defender: Build defensive mechs with long-range weapons. Control the center of the board to block the Attacker's path. Destroy at least one Attacker mech to reduce their options. Building 1 Duo Mech provides a durable roadblock.
medium:
• Attackers: touch + survive 1 Round = destroy.
• Attackers win if all 3 destroyed.
• Defenders win if any survive.
